explanation: This command uses the [char] type with hexadecimal notation, which is directly detected by the Sigma rule. It is a true positive.
This command uses the fully qualified [System.Char] type with hexadecimal notation, which is also detected by the Sigma rule. It is a true positive.

This command uses [char] with decimal notation instead of hexadecimal. Since the Sigma rule specifically looks for '0x' in the command line, this bypasses the detection by omitting the hexadecimal format.
This command uses the [Convert]::ToChar method instead of [char] casting. The Sigma rule does not detect this syntax, making it a potential bypass.
This command converts the hexadecimal value indirectly by converting the string 'A' to an integer using base 16. The Sigma rule does not detect this obfuscated method.
This command uses parentheses to enclose the hexadecimal value. The Sigma rule does not detect this variation of syntax.
